title: Components and JSX
description: CReact components are functions that return JSX. JSX describes resources and processes, not DOM elements.
jsx_intro: CReact uses JSX to describe <0>resources and processes</0>, not DOM elements. The durable counter's <1>App</1> returns a single <2>Counter</2> node that declares the counter should exist.
component_functions_intro: A component is a plain function that returns JSX. It runs a single time as setup. <0>Counter</0> calls <1>useAsyncOutput</1> to own its durable state, wires an effect, and returns. Reactivity comes from signals and effects, never from re-rendering.
composing_intro: Nesting JSX composes real resources into a tree. The uptime monitor's <0>App</0> pairs a <1>For</1> over its targets with a <2>StatusPage</2>, so each probe and the rendered page all live in one component body.
tip_reactive_props: Props can be reactive. Pass an accessor such as <0>overall</0> or a callback such as <1>onSample</1> and children re-run when the value changes, without re-running the parent.
